articles.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission's Phase II, Targets Energy Security and Decarbonization
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and consumption across key sectors.
• This phase introduces new incentives for green hydrogen production, including viability gap funding for electrolysis and a framework for green hydrogen hubs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy independence and net-zero targets.
• Emphasis is placed on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers and associated supply chain components.
• The policy framework also includes provisions for skill development and research & development in green hydrogen technologies.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched to promote green hydrogen production.
Phase II focuses on scaling production, incentives for electrolysis, and green hydrogen hubs.
Aims to make India a global hub for production and export.
Key components include indigenous manufacturing of electrolysers and R&D.
Union Cabinet approval marks a significant policy milestone.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential of green hydrogen for India, considering job creation, export opportunities, and technological advancements.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the role of government policy and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen economy.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen refers specifically to hydrogen produced through electrolysis powered by renewable energy.

articles.headline: Devastating Landslides in Darjeeling District Claim At Least 24 Lives, Highlight Himalayan Vulnerability
articles.explainer:
• Heavy monsoon rains triggered multiple landslides in Darjeeling District, West Bengal, resulting in the tragic loss of at least 24 lives.
• The landslides caused extensive damage to infrastructure, including roads and homes, disrupting connectivity and relief efforts.
• Rescue operations by NDRF and SDRF teams are underway, facing challenges due to difficult terrain and continuous rainfall.
• The incident underscores the increasing vulnerability of the Himalayan region to natural disasters exacerbated by climate change and unplanned development.
• Experts call for stricter building codes, early warning systems, and sustainable land-use planning in ecologically sensitive areas.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Disaster and disaster management
Geography of India and the World
prelimsPoints: 24 people killed in landslides in Darjeeling District, West Bengal.
Triggered by heavy monsoon rains.
NDRF and SDRF involved in rescue operations.
Highlights vulnerability of Himalayan region to climate-induced disasters.
Calls for sustainable land-use planning and early warning systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the increasing frequency and intensity of landslides in the Indian Himalayan region.
Discuss the effectiveness of current disaster management strategies in mitigating the impact of such events and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of climate change and anthropogenic activities in exacerbating natural disasters in ecologically fragile zones.
Propose policy measures for sustainable development and resilience building in hill states like West Bengal.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers navigating through debris from a recent landslide in a hilly region.
articles.trivia: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is often referred to as the 'Toy Train'.

articles.headline: Massive Fire at Amar Chitra Katha Warehouse Destroys Nearly 600,000 Books and Archival Materials
articles.explainer:
• A major fire erupted at a warehouse belonging to Amar Chitra Katha in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra, leading to the destruction of approximately 600,000 books.
• The blaze also consumed invaluable archival materials, including original artworks and manuscripts, representing a significant cultural loss.
• Firefighting operations continued for several days, with multiple brigades working to bring the inferno under control.
• The incident raises concerns about safety protocols and disaster preparedness for cultural heritage institutions and publishers.
• Amar Chitra Katha, known for its iconic comic books on Indian mythology and history, faces a monumental task of recovery and preservation of its legacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Fire at Amar Chitra Katha warehouse in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra.
Nearly 600,000 books and archival materials destroyed.
Significant loss of cultural and historical content.
Amar Chitra Katha is known for Indian mythology and history comics.
Highlights need for better preservation and disaster preparedness for cultural assets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in preserving India's cultural heritage, particularly in the context of privately held archives and collections.
Analyze the importance of digital archiving and robust disaster management plans for cultural institutions.
Examine the role of Amar Chitra Katha in shaping popular understanding of Indian history and mythology, and the impact of such a loss.
Suggest measures to safeguard valuable literary and artistic archives from potential disasters.
articles.imageDescription: Smoke billowing from a large warehouse fire, with fire trucks visible in the foreground.
articles.trivia: Amar Chitra Katha was founded in 1967 by Anant Pai, often referred to as 'Uncle Pai'.

articles.headline: Tragic Fire at Sawai Man Singh Hospital ICU in Jaipur Claims Six Lives, Raises Patient Safety Concerns
articles.explainer:
• A devastating fire broke out in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of Sawai Man Singh (SMS) Hospital in Jaipur, resulting in the deaths of at least six patients.
• The fire, suspected to be caused by an electrical short circuit, led to rapid smoke inhalation and critical injuries among patients.
• Authorities have ordered a high-level inquiry into the incident, focusing on fire safety compliance, infrastructure maintenance, and emergency response protocols.
• The tragedy highlights persistent challenges in public healthcare infrastructure, particularly concerning fire safety audits and patient evacuation plans.
• Calls for immediate review of fire safety measures across all public hospitals to prevent similar incidents in the future.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Six patients killed in a fire at SMS Hospital ICU in Jaipur.
Suspected cause: electrical short circuit.
High-level inquiry ordered into fire safety compliance.
Raises concerns about fire safety in public hospitals.
SMS Hospital is a prominent government hospital in Rajasthan.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the state of fire safety infrastructure and emergency preparedness in public hospitals across India.
Discuss the regulatory mechanisms and enforcement challenges related to fire safety standards in healthcare facilities.
Suggest comprehensive reforms required to enhance patient safety and prevent fire-related tragedies in hospitals.
Examine the ethical and governance implications of such incidents on public trust in healthcare systems.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services personnel outside a hospital building, with a portion of the building showing signs of fire damage.
articles.trivia: Sawai Man Singh Hospital is one of the largest government hospitals in Rajasthan, named after Maharaja Sawai Man Singh II.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to inflation management while supporting economic growth, amidst global uncertainties and domestic inflation pressures.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target, while revising GDP growth projections upwards.
• The central bank highlighted risks from volatile global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions, alongside robust domestic demand.
• Experts suggest the hold indicates a period of 'wait and watch' before any potential rate cuts, dependent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Sixth consecutive time the rate has been held.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
GDP growth projections revised upwards, inflation concerns persist.
Decision influenced by global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic activity.
Examine the impact of global economic headwinds and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving sustained disinflation.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: ISRO Announces Preparations for Chandrayaan-4, Eyes Lunar Sample Return Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) officially announced the commencement of preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis.
• This mission will significantly advance India's capabilities in deep space exploration and planetary science.
• The mission profile is expected to involve a lander, rover, and an ascent module to collect samples, followed by an orbital module for Earth return.
• This follows the successes of Chandrayaan-1, 2, and 3, building on India's growing prowess in lunar exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4 announced by ISRO.
Aims to be a lunar sample return mission.
Will collect lunar soil and rock samples for analysis.
Includes lander, rover, ascent module, and Earth return module.
Follows previous successful Chandrayaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze how Chandrayaan-4 will enhance India's capabilities in deep space exploration and its standing in the global space community.
Examine the technological challenges involved in executing a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is preparing to overcome them.
Evaluate the broader implications of such missions for indigenous technology development and scientific research in India.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of a lunar lander and rover on the Moon's surface, collecting samples.
articles.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: the USA, Russia (then Soviet Union), and China.

articles.headline: Padma Vibhushan Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, Veteran Hindustani Classical Vocalist, Passes Away at 89
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Hindustani classical vocalist and Padma Vibhushan recipient, Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, passed away at the age of 89.
• A doyen of the Kirana Gharana, Mishra was celebrated for his mastery of Khayal, Thumri, Dadra, and Kajri forms.
• His demise marks the end of an era in Indian classical music, leaving behind a rich legacy of musical contributions and disciples.
• Mishra was honored with the Padma Bhushan in 2010 and Padma Vibhushan in 2016 for his exceptional contributions to Indian arts.
• His music transcended boundaries, enchanting audiences globally and promoting Indian classical traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, renowned Hindustani classical vocalist, passed away.
Belonged to the Kirana Gharana.
Mastered Khayal, Thumri, Dadra, and Kajri.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan (2010) and Padma Vibhushan (2016).
His death is a significant loss to Indian classical music.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and distinctive features of the Kirana Gharana in Hindustani classical music, with reference to artists like Pandit Chhannulal Mishra.
Analyze the role of classical musicians in preserving and promoting India's cultural heritage in a contemporary context.
Examine the significance of national awards like Padma Vibhushan in recognizing and encouraging contributions to arts and culture.
Discuss the challenges faced by traditional art forms in modern times and strategies for their sustenance.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Pandit Chhannulal Mishra performing, with a microphone in front of him.
articles.trivia: The Kirana Gharana is one of the oldest and most influential gharanas of Hindustani classical music, known for its emphasis on melodic elaboration and emotional expression.

articles.headline: Former MLAs Ramreddy Damodar Reddy and Rameshwar Lal Dudi, Ex-Speaker Giriraj Prasad Tiwari Pass Away
articles.explainer:
• The political fraternity mourned the passing of several prominent leaders: Ramreddy Damodar Reddy (73), Giriraj Prasad Tiwari (104), and Rameshwar Lal Dudi (62).
• Ramreddy Damodar Reddy was a veteran politician from Andhra Pradesh, serving multiple terms as an MLA from 1985-1999 and 2004-2014.
• Giriraj Prasad Tiwari was a centenarian leader and former Speaker of the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly (1986–1990), known for his long political career.
• Rameshwar Lal Dudi, a former MP (1999–2004) and Rajasthan MLA (2013–2018), was a significant figure in Rajasthan politics.
• Their contributions spanned decades, influencing state and national legislative processes and public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Ramreddy Damodar Reddy (Andhra Pradesh MLA), Giriraj Prasad Tiwari (Rajasthan Assembly Speaker), and Rameshwar Lal Dudi (MP, Rajasthan MLA) passed away.
Reddy served as MLA from 1985-1999, 2004-2014.
Tiwari was Speaker of Rajasthan Legislative Assembly from 1986-1990.
Dudi served as MP from 1999-2004 and Rajasthan MLA from 2013-2018.
Represented significant political careers in their respective states.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of state legislative assemblies and their speakers in India's parliamentary democracy.
Analyze the contributions of long-serving politicians to regional development and national policy-making.
Examine the significance of political leadership in shaping governance and public representation at state and national levels.
Reflect on the legacy of veteran politicians in strengthening democratic institutions.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of portraits of the three deceased politicians.
articles.trivia: The Speaker of a State Legislative Assembly is the presiding officer and guardian of the powers and privileges of the House.

articles.headline: Veteran Journalist and Biographer T. J. S. George Passes Away at 97
articles.explainer:
• T. J. S. George, a towering figure in Indian journalism and an acclaimed biographer, passed away at the age of 97.
• Known for his incisive writing and fearless journalism, George had a career spanning over six decades, contributing to numerous national and international publications.
• He was particularly celebrated for his biographies of prominent personalities, offering deep insights into their lives and times.
• George was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian award, for his literary and journalistic contributions.
• His work significantly influenced political commentary and literary discourse in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of media and social networking sites in Indian democracy
prelimsPoints: T. J. S. George, veteran journalist and biographer, passed away.
Known for incisive writing and biographies.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan.
Career spanned over six decades.
Influenced political commentary and literary discourse.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of biographical writing in India and its role in historical documentation and cultural understanding.
Analyze the impact of veteran journalists like T.J.S. George on the standards and ethics of Indian media.
Examine the challenges faced by independent journalism in the contemporary media landscape.
Reflect on the significance of literary contributions in shaping public opinion and historical narratives.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of T. J. S. George, a distinguished gentleman in his later years.
articles.trivia: T. J. S. George was the founding editor of the 'Asiaweek' magazine, based in Hong Kong.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Regional Stability and Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded with a joint statement emphasizing enhanced cooperation for regional peace and stability.
• Discussions focused on addressing traditional and non-traditional maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Both sides stressed the importance of adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS, in resolving maritime disputes.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and offered capacity-building support to ASEAN member states.
• The dialogue is crucial for strengthening strategic partnerships and ensuring secure sea lanes of communication in the critical Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded.
Focused on regional peace, stability, and maritime threats.
Stressed adherence to international law, especially UNCLOS.
India committed to free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Aims to strengthen strategic partnerships and secure sea lanes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its engagement with ASEAN member states on maritime issues.
Examine the role of international law, particularly UNCLOS, in managing maritime disputes and promoting stability in the region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's 'Act East' policy in fostering deeper security and economic ties with Southeast Asian nations.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and ASEAN nations conducting a joint maritime exercise in the open sea.
articles.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five founding members.

articles.headline: Government Rolls Out 'Digital Seva 2.0' Platform to Enhance Citizen Service Delivery and e-Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology launched 'Digital Seva 2.0', an upgraded e-governance platform aimed at improving citizen service delivery.
• The new platform integrates AI-powered chatbots and advanced data analytics to provide more personalized and efficient government services.
• It expands the scope of services available online, including enhanced features for land records, health services, and social welfare schemes.
• Digital Seva 2.0 focuses on ensuring greater transparency, accountability, and accessibility of government services, particularly in rural areas.
• The initiative aligns with the vision of 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' and aims to bridge the digital divide.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Digital Seva 2.0 launched by MeitY.
Upgraded e-governance platform for citizen service delivery.
Integrates AI chatbots and data analytics.
Expands online services for land records, health, social welfare.
Aims for transparency, accountability, and accessibility, especially in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the potential of e-governance platforms like 'Digital Seva 2.0' in enhancing public service delivery and promoting transparency in India.
Discuss the challenges associated with implementing advanced digital solutions in governance, particularly concerning digital literacy, data privacy, and infrastructure in rural areas.
Analyze how AI and data analytics can transform governance and improve citizen-centric services.
Examine the steps needed to ensure equitable access to digital services and bridge the digital divide in India.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital services emanating from a government portal.
articles.trivia: The 'Digital India' program was launched in 2015 with the vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Expands to Include New Reserve in Eastern Ghats, Boosting Biodiversity Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The government announced the inclusion of a new tiger reserve in the Eastern Ghats region, marking a significant expansion of Project Tiger.
• This new reserve aims to protect a crucial tiger corridor and enhance the conservation of biodiversity in the less-explored Eastern Ghats.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict in existing reserves.
• The new reserve will involve local community participation in conservation efforts and promote eco-tourism sustainably.
• Project Tiger continues to be a flagship conservation program, contributing to a steady increase in India's tiger population.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New tiger reserve established in Eastern Ghats under Project Tiger.
Aims to protect a crucial tiger corridor and biodiversity.
Addresses habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict.
Involves local community participation and sustainable eco-tourism.
Project Tiger is India's flagship tiger conservation program.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of expanding protected areas like tiger reserves for biodiversity conservation in India.
Discuss the ecological significance of the Eastern Ghats as a potential habitat for diverse wildlife, including tigers.
Examine the challenges in managing human-wildlife conflict and ensuring community participation in conservation efforts.
Evaluate the success of Project Tiger in India and suggest future strategies for sustainable wildlife management.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage in a protected reserve.
articles.trivia: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 and has been instrumental in increasing India's tiger population from a mere 1,800 in the 1970s to over 3,000 currently.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Guidelines on Reservation in Promotions for SC/ST Employees
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated and clarified existing guidelines regarding reservation in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) in government jobs.
• The judgment emphasized the need for quantifiable data to justify reservation in promotions, ensuring efficiency in administration is not compromised.
• It stressed that the state is not bound to provide reservation in promotions but if it chooses to do so, it must fulfill certain conditions, including backwardness and inadequate representation.
• The ruling aims to strike a balance between affirmative action for marginalized communities and the principle of administrative efficiency.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for public sector employment policies across various states and central ministries.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court clarified guidelines on reservation in promotions for SC/ST.
Emphasized need for quantifiable data to justify such reservations.
State not bound, but must meet conditions (backwardness, inadequate representation) if provided.
Aims to balance affirmative action and administrative efficiency.
Impacts public sector employment policies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions and judicial pronouncements governing reservation in promotions for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in India.
Discuss the complexities involved in balancing the principles of social justice, administrative efficiency, and meritocracy in public employment.
Examine the socio-economic implications of reservation policies on vulnerable sections and their role in achieving equity.
Critically evaluate the arguments for and against reservation in promotions, considering the need for quantifiable data and periodic review.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial decisions.
articles.trivia: Article 16(4A) and 16(4B) of the Indian Constitution deal with reservation in promotions for SCs and STs.

articles.headline: Indian Army and US Special Forces Conclude Joint Exercise 'Vajra Prahar' in Himachal Pradesh
articles.explainer:
• The 16th edition of the joint military exercise 'Vajra Prahar' between Indian Army Special Forces and US Special Forces concluded in Himachal Pradesh.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability and sharing best practices in counter-terrorism operations, special operations, and airborne assaults.
• It involved complex tactical drills, hostage rescue scenarios, and precision targeting in mountainous terrain.
• 'Vajra Prahar' serves as a crucial platform for strengthening defense cooperation and strategic partnership between India and the United States.
• The exercise underscores the growing convergence of security interests between the two nations in the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: 'Vajra Prahar' is a joint exercise between Indian Army and US Special Forces.
16th edition held in Himachal Pradesh.
Focus on counter-terrorism, special operations, airborne assaults.
Enhances interoperability and defense cooperation.
Strengthens India-US strategic partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of joint military exercises like 'Vajra Prahar' in strengthening India's defense capabilities and fostering international security cooperation.
Discuss how such exercises contribute to enhancing interoperability and sharing best practices in counter-terrorism operations.
Examine the evolving defense partnership between India and the United States and its implications for regional and global security.
Evaluate the role of special forces in modern warfare and their importance in tackling non-traditional security threats.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and US Special Forces soldiers conducting a tactical drill in mountainous terrain during the 'Vajra Prahar' exercise.
articles.trivia: The name 'Vajra Prahar' translates to 'Thunderbolt Strike' in Sanskrit.

articles.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Mega Industrial Corridor Project in Western India, Boosting Manufacturing and Logistics
articles.explainer:
• A major segment of a new mega industrial corridor project was inaugurated by the Union Minister for Commerce and Industry in Western India.
• The corridor aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, promoting manufacturing, logistics, and job creation in the region.
• It features state-of-the-art infrastructure, including dedicated freight corridors, smart industrial zones, and multimodal logistics hubs.
• The project is part of India's broader strategy to enhance its manufacturing competitiveness and integrate into global supply chains.
• Expected to boost regional economic development and improve ease of doing business for industries.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Mega industrial corridor project inaugurated in Western India.
Aims to attract investment, boost manufacturing and logistics.
Features dedicated freight corridors, smart industrial zones, multimodal hubs.
Part of India's strategy to enhance manufacturing competitiveness.
Expected to boost regional economic development and job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of industrial corridors in driving economic growth, promoting manufacturing, and creating employment opportunities in India.
Analyze the role of infrastructure development in attracting foreign direct investment (FDI) and integrating India into global supply chains.
Examine the challenges and potential benefits of developing mega infrastructure projects, including land acquisition and environmental concerns.
Evaluate the 'Make in India' initiative in the context of industrial corridor development and its impact on manufacturing competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a sprawling industrial zone with factories and interconnected roads.
articles.trivia: India's first industrial corridor was the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC), conceived to create smart industrial cities along the route.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh, Offering New Historical Insights
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in a remote region of Ladakh.
• The excavation revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts dating back to the 8th-9th century CE, shedding new light on early Buddhist presence in the Himalayas.
• The findings suggest a thriving Buddhist cultural and religious center that predates many known monasteries in the region.
• This discovery holds immense archaeological and historical importance, contributing to the understanding of trans-Himalayan cultural exchanges.
• Further studies are underway to preserve the site and conduct detailed research on the unearthed relics and architecture.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Ladakh.
Dates back to 8th-9th century CE.
Revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts.
Suggests early Buddhist presence in the Himalayas.
Significant for understanding trans-Himalayan cultural exchanges.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism in India, particularly in remote regions like Ladakh.
Analyze how such findings contribute to our understanding of cultural and religious exchanges across the Himalayas in ancient times.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and documenting India's tangible cultural heritage.
Suggest measures for the sustainable conservation and promotion of newly discovered archaeological sites.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ruins of an ancient stone structure in a desolate mountainous landscape.
articles.trivia: Ladakh is often called 'Little Tibet' due to its strong cultural and religious ties with Tibetan Buddhism.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Major Shortfall in Global Climate Finance for Developing Nations
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) warns of a significant shortfall in climate finance for developing countries.
• The report states that developed nations are falling short of their commitment to provide $100 billion annually, hindering climate action in vulnerable regions.
• It emphasizes the urgent need for increased public and private funding to support adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing economies.
• The shortfall disproportionately affects countries most vulnerable to climate impacts, exacerbating existing inequalities.
• Calls for innovative financing mechanisms and greater transparency in climate finance flows ahead of the next COP summit.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNFCCC report highlights shortfall in global climate finance for developing nations.
Developed nations falling short of $100 billion annual commitment.
Urgent need for increased public and private funding.
Disproportionately affects vulnerable countries.
Calls for innovative financing and transparency before next COP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and practical implications of the shortfall in climate finance for developing countries, particularly India.
Analyze the mechanisms and challenges associated with mobilizing and delivering climate finance to vulnerable nations.
Examine India's position and efforts in advocating for greater climate justice and equitable burden-sharing in global climate action.
Suggest innovative financing models and policy reforms to bridge the climate finance gap and accelerate climate action globally.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the gap between committed and delivered climate finance, with scales tipping unevenly.
articles.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at the 2009 UN Climate Change Conference (COP15) in Copenhagen.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Reviews Progress of National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee convened to review the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the integration of vocational education, changes in curriculum design, and the establishment of new regulatory bodies.
• Members expressed concerns regarding funding allocation, teacher training, and equitable access to quality education, especially in remote areas.
• The panel emphasized the need for a phased and inclusive approach to NEP implementation, ensuring no student is left behind.
• Recommendations are expected to be submitted to the government to address bottlenecks and accelerate the policy's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee reviewed NEP 2020 implementation.
Focused on vocational education, curriculum, regulatory bodies.
Concerns raised on funding, teacher training, equitable access.
Emphasized phased and inclusive approach.
Recommendations to address bottlenecks and accelerate NEP objectives.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and their potential impact on India's education system.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education policy like NEP 2020 across diverse states and educational institutions.
Examine the importance of vocational education and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP and enhancing human resource development.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and bridge the learning gap in India, especially in remote and disadvantaged regions.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after policies in 1968 and 1986 (modified in 1992).

articles.headline: FAO Food Price Index Sees Modest Rise, Global Food Security Remains Fragile
articles.explainer:
• The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) released its monthly Food Price Index, indicating a modest rise driven by increases in dairy and sugar prices.
• Despite the slight increase, the overall index remains below its peak levels from previous years, but global food security remains fragile.
• The report highlights ongoing concerns about geopolitical conflicts, climate shocks, and supply chain disruptions affecting food availability and access.
• Developing nations, particularly in Africa and Asia, continue to face acute food insecurity challenges.
• Calls for enhanced international cooperation and resilient agricultural systems to ensure stable food supplies globally.
gsSubTopics: Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: FAO Food Price Index shows modest rise, driven by dairy and sugar.
Global food security remains fragile despite index below peak.
Geopolitical conflicts, climate shocks, supply chain disruptions are concerns.
Developing nations face acute food insecurity.
Calls for international cooperation and resilient agricultural systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the volatility of global food prices and their impact on food security in India and other developing nations.
Discuss the challenges faced by India in ensuring food security for its large population amidst climate change and global supply chain disruptions.
Examine the role of international organizations like FAO in monitoring global food prices and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
Suggest policy interventions at national and international levels to build resilient food systems and address food insecurity effectively.
articles.imageDescription: Various fresh food items arranged on a table, representing global food supply.
articles.trivia: The FAO Food Price Index is a measure of the monthly change in international prices of a basket of food commodities.

articles.headline: G20 Tourism Ministers Adopt 'Goa Roadmap' for Sustainable Tourism Development
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Tourism Ministers' Meeting concluded with the adoption of the 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism', focusing on sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
• The roadmap outlines key priorities, including green tourism, digitalization, skill development, and promotion of cultural heritage.
• Ministers emphasized the importance of resilient tourism systems capable of withstanding future shocks and contributing to local economies.
• India, as the host, championed the concept of 'Travel for LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) to promote responsible tourism practices.
• The declaration aims to foster international cooperation to rebuild and reshape the global tourism sector for a more sustainable future.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: G20 Tourism Ministers adopted 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism'.
Focus on sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
Priorities: green tourism, digitalization, skill development, cultural heritage.
India championed 'Travel for LiFE'.
Aims to foster international cooperation for resilient tourism.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism' in promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices globally and for India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging its cultural heritage and natural beauty for sustainable tourism development.
Examine the role of digitalization and skill development in transforming the tourism sector and enhancing its resilience to future crises.
Evaluate the 'Travel for LiFE' initiative and its potential to encourage environmentally conscious tourism behaviors among travelers.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of a beach in Goa with tourists enjoying, symbolizing tourism and leisure.
articles.trivia: India hosted the G20 presidency in 2023, during which several key initiatives, including the 'Goa Roadmap', were finalized.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission to boost energy security and decarbonization.
Devastating landslides in Darjeeling District claim at least 24 lives, highlighting Himalayan region's vulnerability.
Massive fire at Amar Chitra Katha warehouse destroys 600,000 books and archival materials, a significant cultural loss.
Tragic fire at Jaipur's Sawai Man Singh Hospital ICU kills six patients, raising critical patient safety concerns.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signalling cautious optimism amidst global economic headwinds.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission primarily aims to promote blue hydrogen production in India.
2. Phase II of the mission includes viability gap funding for electrolysis projects.
3. The mission seeks to make India a net importer of green hydrogen.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The mission primarily aims to promote *green* hydrogen production, which is produced using renewable energy, not blue hydrogen (produced from natural gas with carbon capture). Statement 2 is correct: Phase II introduces incentives like viability gap funding for electrolysis. Statement 3 is incorrect: The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and *export*, not a net importer.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the recent landslides in Darjeeling District is/are correct?
1. The landslides were triggered by heavy monsoon rains.
2. The incident highlights the ecological resilience of the Himalayan region.
3. NDRF and SDRF teams are involved in rescue operations.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The landslides were triggered by heavy monsoon rains. Statement 2 is incorrect: The incident *underscores the increasing vulnerability* of the Himalayan region, not its resilience. Statement 3 is correct: NDRF and SDRF teams are involved in rescue operations.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the passing of Pandit Chhannulal Mishra:
1. He was a veteran Hindustani classical vocalist.
2. He belonged to the Gwalior Gharana.
3. He was a recipient of the Padma Vibhushan.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Pandit Chhannulal Mishra was a renowned Hindustani classical vocalist. Statement 2 is incorrect: He belonged to the *Kirana Gharana*, not Gwalior Gharana. Statement 3 is correct: He was honored with the Padma Vibhushan in 2016.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Vajra Prahar' is a joint military exercise conducted between the Special Forces of India and which other country?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Vajra Prahar' joint military exercise is conducted between the Indian Army Special Forces and US Special Forces to enhance interoperability and share best practices in counter-terrorism and special operations.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Supreme Court's guidelines on reservation in promotions for SC/ST employees?
1. The State is constitutionally bound to provide reservation in promotions.
2. Quantifiable data is required to justify such reservations.
3. The ruling aims to balance affirmative action with administrative efficiency.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The court stressed that the state is *not bound* to provide reservation in promotions. Statement 2 is correct: The judgment emphasized the need for quantifiable data. Statement 3 is correct: The ruling aims to strike a balance between affirmative action and administrative efficiency.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by India in achieving its ambitious green hydrogen targets. What policy interventions are crucial to overcome these hurdles and establish India as a global leader in green hydrogen production? (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission's significance. List challenges like high production costs, lack of infrastructure, technology gaps, and water availability. Suggest policy interventions such as subsidies, R&D funding, infrastructure development, international collaborations, and regulatory clarity.

mainsMocks.question: The recent landslides in Darjeeling highlight the increasing vulnerability of the Himalayan region to natural disasters. Analyze the anthropogenic and natural factors contributing to such events. What comprehensive strategies, including disaster management and sustainable development, are required for enhancing resilience in these ecologically fragile zones? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Important Geophysical phenomena; GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the Darjeeling tragedy. Discuss natural factors (heavy rainfall, seismic activity, fragile geology) and anthropogenic factors (deforestation, unplanned construction, road building, climate change impacts). Propose comprehensive strategies: early warning systems, strict land-use planning, eco-friendly infrastructure, community-based disaster preparedness, afforestation, and climate change adaptation measures.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate the significance of e-governance platforms like 'Digital Seva 2.0' in transforming public service delivery and promoting transparency in India. What are the key challenges in ensuring equitable access and effective utilization of such digital initiatives across the country? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Digital Seva 2.0' and its goals (efficiency, transparency, accessibility). Discuss its significance in reducing corruption, enhancing citizen convenience, and improving governance. Identify challenges: digital literacy gap, internet connectivity issues in rural areas, data privacy and security concerns, resistance to change, and ensuring inclusivity for marginalized sections. Suggest solutions like digital literacy programs, robust infrastructure, and user-friendly interfaces.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Darjeeling District, located in West Bengal, is part of the Lesser Himalayan range.
Amar Chitra Katha was founded by Anant Pai in 1967, known for Indian mythology and history comics.
The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members.
ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 mission is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission, following Chandrayaan-3's success.
Pandit Chhannulal Mishra was a prominent exponent of the Kirana Gharana in Hindustani classical music.
Article 16(4A) and 16(4B) of the Indian Constitution deal with reservation in promotions for SCs and STs.
The United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) is a key framework for maritime disputes.
Project Tiger, launched in 1973, is India's flagship program for tiger conservation.
The 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism' was adopted by G20 Tourism Ministers, promoting sustainable tourism.

GS1.headline: Devastating Landslides in Darjeeling District Claim At Least 24 Lives, Highlight Himalayan Vulnerability
GS1.explainer:
• Heavy monsoon rains triggered multiple landslides in Darjeeling District, West Bengal, resulting in the tragic loss of at least 24 lives.
• The landslides caused extensive damage to infrastructure, including roads and homes, disrupting connectivity and relief efforts.
• Rescue operations by NDRF and SDRF teams are underway, facing challenges due to difficult terrain and continuous rainfall.
• The incident underscores the increasing vulnerability of the Himalayan region to natural disasters exacerbated by climate change and unplanned development.
• Experts call for stricter building codes, early warning systems, and sustainable land-use planning in ecologically sensitive areas.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Disaster and disaster management
Geography of India and the World
prelimsPoints: 24 people killed in landslides in Darjeeling District, West Bengal.
Triggered by heavy monsoon rains.
NDRF and SDRF involved in rescue operations.
Highlights vulnerability of Himalayan region to climate-induced disasters.
Calls for sustainable land-use planning and early warning systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the increasing frequency and intensity of landslides in the Indian Himalayan region.
Discuss the effectiveness of current disaster management strategies in mitigating the impact of such events and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of climate change and anthropogenic activities in exacerbating natural disasters in ecologically fragile zones.
Propose policy measures for sustainable development and resilience building in hill states like West Bengal.
GS1.imageDescription: Rescue workers navigating through debris from a recent landslide in a hilly region.
GS1.trivia: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is often referred to as the 'Toy Train'.

GS1.headline: Massive Fire at Amar Chitra Katha Warehouse Destroys Nearly 600,000 Books and Archival Materials
GS1.explainer:
• A major fire erupted at a warehouse belonging to Amar Chitra Katha in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra, leading to the destruction of approximately 600,000 books.
• The blaze also consumed invaluable archival materials, including original artworks and manuscripts, representing a significant cultural loss.
• Firefighting operations continued for several days, with multiple brigades working to bring the inferno under control.
• The incident raises concerns about safety protocols and disaster preparedness for cultural heritage institutions and publishers.
• Amar Chitra Katha, known for its iconic comic books on Indian mythology and history, faces a monumental task of recovery and preservation of its legacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Fire at Amar Chitra Katha warehouse in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra.
Nearly 600,000 books and archival materials destroyed.
Significant loss of cultural and historical content.
Amar Chitra Katha is known for Indian mythology and history comics.
Highlights need for better preservation and disaster preparedness for cultural assets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in preserving India's cultural heritage, particularly in the context of privately held archives and collections.
Analyze the importance of digital archiving and robust disaster management plans for cultural institutions.
Examine the role of Amar Chitra Katha in shaping popular understanding of Indian history and mythology, and the impact of such a loss.
Suggest measures to safeguard valuable literary and artistic archives from potential disasters.
GS1.imageDescription: Smoke billowing from a large warehouse fire, with fire trucks visible in the foreground.
GS1.trivia: Amar Chitra Katha was founded in 1967 by Anant Pai, often referred to as 'Uncle Pai'.

GS1.headline: Padma Vibhushan Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, Veteran Hindustani Classical Vocalist, Passes Away at 89
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Hindustani classical vocalist and Padma Vibhushan recipient, Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, passed away at the age of 89.
• A doyen of the Kirana Gharana, Mishra was celebrated for his mastery of Khayal, Thumri, Dadra, and Kajri forms.
• His demise marks the end of an era in Indian classical music, leaving behind a rich legacy of musical contributions and disciples.
• Mishra was honored with the Padma Bhushan in 2010 and Padma Vibhushan in 2016 for his exceptional contributions to Indian arts.
• His music transcended boundaries, enchanting audiences globally and promoting Indian classical traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Pandit Chhannulal Mishra, renowned Hindustani classical vocalist, passed away.
Belonged to the Kirana Gharana.
Mastered Khayal, Thumri, Dadra, and Kajri.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan (2010) and Padma Vibhushan (2016).
His death is a significant loss to Indian classical music.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and distinctive features of the Kirana Gharana in Hindustani classical music, with reference to artists like Pandit Chhannulal Mishra.
Analyze the role of classical musicians in preserving and promoting India's cultural heritage in a contemporary context.
Examine the significance of national awards like Padma Vibhushan in recognizing and encouraging contributions to arts and culture.
Discuss the challenges faced by traditional art forms in modern times and strategies for their sustenance.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Pandit Chhannulal Mishra performing, with a microphone in front of him.
GS1.trivia: The Kirana Gharana is one of the oldest and most influential gharanas of Hindustani classical music, known for its emphasis on melodic elaboration and emotional expression.

GS1.headline: Veteran Journalist and Biographer T. J. S. George Passes Away at 97
GS1.explainer:
• T. J. S. George, a towering figure in Indian journalism and an acclaimed biographer, passed away at the age of 97.
• Known for his incisive writing and fearless journalism, George had a career spanning over six decades, contributing to numerous national and international publications.
• He was particularly celebrated for his biographies of prominent personalities, offering deep insights into their lives and times.
• George was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian award, for his literary and journalistic contributions.
• His work significantly influenced political commentary and literary discourse in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of media and social networking sites in Indian democracy
prelimsPoints: T. J. S. George, veteran journalist and biographer, passed away.
Known for incisive writing and biographies.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan.
Career spanned over six decades.
Influenced political commentary and literary discourse.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of biographical writing in India and its role in historical documentation and cultural understanding.
Analyze the impact of veteran journalists like T.J.S. George on the standards and ethics of Indian media.
Examine the challenges faced by independent journalism in the contemporary media landscape.
Reflect on the significance of literary contributions in shaping public opinion and historical narratives.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of T. J. S. George, a distinguished gentleman in his later years.
GS1.trivia: T. J. S. George was the founding editor of the 'Asiaweek' magazine, based in Hong Kong.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh, Offering New Historical Insights
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in a remote region of Ladakh.
• The excavation revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts dating back to the 8th-9th century CE, shedding new light on early Buddhist presence in the Himalayas.
• The findings suggest a thriving Buddhist cultural and religious center that predates many known monasteries in the region.
• This discovery holds immense archaeological and historical importance, contributing to the understanding of trans-Himalayan cultural exchanges.
• Further studies are underway to preserve the site and conduct detailed research on the unearthed relics and architecture.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Ladakh.
Dates back to 8th-9th century CE.
Revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts.
Suggests early Buddhist presence in the Himalayas.
Significant for understanding trans-Himalayan cultural exchanges.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism in India, particularly in remote regions like Ladakh.
Analyze how such findings contribute to our understanding of cultural and religious exchanges across the Himalayas in ancient times.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and documenting India's tangible cultural heritage.
Suggest measures for the sustainable conservation and promotion of newly discovered archaeological sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ruins of an ancient stone structure in a desolate mountainous landscape.
GS1.trivia: Ladakh is often called 'Little Tibet' due to its strong cultural and religious ties with Tibetan Buddhism.

GS2.headline: Tragic Fire at Sawai Man Singh Hospital ICU in Jaipur Claims Six Lives, Raises Patient Safety Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• A devastating fire broke out in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of Sawai Man Singh (SMS) Hospital in Jaipur, resulting in the deaths of at least six patients.
• The fire, suspected to be caused by an electrical short circuit, led to rapid smoke inhalation and critical injuries among patients.
• Authorities have ordered a high-level inquiry into the incident, focusing on fire safety compliance, infrastructure maintenance, and emergency response protocols.
• The tragedy highlights persistent challenges in public healthcare infrastructure, particularly concerning fire safety audits and patient evacuation plans.
• Calls for immediate review of fire safety measures across all public hospitals to prevent similar incidents in the future.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Six patients killed in a fire at SMS Hospital ICU in Jaipur.
Suspected cause: electrical short circuit.
High-level inquiry ordered into fire safety compliance.
Raises concerns about fire safety in public hospitals.
SMS Hospital is a prominent government hospital in Rajasthan.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the state of fire safety infrastructure and emergency preparedness in public hospitals across India.
Discuss the regulatory mechanisms and enforcement challenges related to fire safety standards in healthcare facilities.
Suggest comprehensive reforms required to enhance patient safety and prevent fire-related tragedies in hospitals.
Examine the ethical and governance implications of such incidents on public trust in healthcare systems.
GS2.imageDescription: Emergency services personnel outside a hospital building, with a portion of the building showing signs of fire damage.
GS2.trivia: Sawai Man Singh Hospital is one of the largest government hospitals in Rajasthan, named after Maharaja Sawai Man Singh II.

GS2.headline: Former MLAs Ramreddy Damodar Reddy and Rameshwar Lal Dudi, Ex-Speaker Giriraj Prasad Tiwari Pass Away
GS2.explainer:
• The political fraternity mourned the passing of several prominent leaders: Ramreddy Damodar Reddy (73), Giriraj Prasad Tiwari (104), and Rameshwar Lal Dudi (62).
• Ramreddy Damodar Reddy was a veteran politician from Andhra Pradesh, serving multiple terms as an MLA from 1985-1999 and 2004-2014.
• Giriraj Prasad Tiwari was a centenarian leader and former Speaker of the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly (1986–1990), known for his long political career.
• Rameshwar Lal Dudi, a former MP (1999–2004) and Rajasthan MLA (2013–2018), was a significant figure in Rajasthan politics.
• Their contributions spanned decades, influencing state and national legislative processes and public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Ramreddy Damodar Reddy (Andhra Pradesh MLA), Giriraj Prasad Tiwari (Rajasthan Assembly Speaker), and Rameshwar Lal Dudi (MP, Rajasthan MLA) passed away.
Reddy served as MLA from 1985-1999, 2004-2014.
Tiwari was Speaker of Rajasthan Legislative Assembly from 1986-1990.
Dudi served as MP from 1999-2004 and Rajasthan MLA from 2013-2018.
Represented significant political careers in their respective states.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of state legislative assemblies and their speakers in India's parliamentary democracy.
Analyze the contributions of long-serving politicians to regional development and national policy-making.
Examine the significance of political leadership in shaping governance and public representation at state and national levels.
Reflect on the legacy of veteran politicians in strengthening democratic institutions.
GS2.imageDescription: A collage of portraits of the three deceased politicians.
GS2.trivia: The Speaker of a State Legislative Assembly is the presiding officer and guardian of the powers and privileges of the House.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Regional Stability and Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded with a joint statement emphasizing enhanced cooperation for regional peace and stability.
• Discussions focused on addressing traditional and non-traditional maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Both sides stressed the importance of adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS, in resolving maritime disputes.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and offered capacity-building support to ASEAN member states.
• The dialogue is crucial for strengthening strategic partnerships and ensuring secure sea lanes of communication in the critical Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded.
Focused on regional peace, stability, and maritime threats.
Stressed adherence to international law, especially UNCLOS.
India committed to free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Aims to strengthen strategic partnerships and secure sea lanes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its engagement with ASEAN member states on maritime issues.
Examine the role of international law, particularly UNCLOS, in managing maritime disputes and promoting stability in the region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's 'Act East' policy in fostering deeper security and economic ties with Southeast Asian nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and ASEAN nations conducting a joint maritime exercise in the open sea.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five founding members.

GS2.headline: Government Rolls Out 'Digital Seva 2.0' Platform to Enhance Citizen Service Delivery and e-Governance
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology launched 'Digital Seva 2.0', an upgraded e-governance platform aimed at improving citizen service delivery.
• The new platform integrates AI-powered chatbots and advanced data analytics to provide more personalized and efficient government services.
• It expands the scope of services available online, including enhanced features for land records, health services, and social welfare schemes.
• Digital Seva 2.0 focuses on ensuring greater transparency, accountability, and accessibility of government services, particularly in rural areas.
• The initiative aligns with the vision of 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' and aims to bridge the digital divide.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Digital Seva 2.0 launched by MeitY.
Upgraded e-governance platform for citizen service delivery.
Integrates AI chatbots and data analytics.
Expands online services for land records, health, social welfare.
Aims for transparency, accountability, and accessibility, especially in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the potential of e-governance platforms like 'Digital Seva 2.0' in enhancing public service delivery and promoting transparency in India.
Discuss the challenges associated with implementing advanced digital solutions in governance, particularly concerning digital literacy, data privacy, and infrastructure in rural areas.
Analyze how AI and data analytics can transform governance and improve citizen-centric services.
Examine the steps needed to ensure equitable access to digital services and bridge the digital divide in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital services emanating from a government portal.
GS2.trivia: The 'Digital India' program was launched in 2015 with the vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Guidelines on Reservation in Promotions for SC/ST Employees
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated and clarified existing guidelines regarding reservation in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) in government jobs.
• The judgment emphasized the need for quantifiable data to justify reservation in promotions, ensuring efficiency in administration is not compromised.
• It stressed that the state is not bound to provide reservation in promotions but if it chooses to do so, it must fulfill certain conditions, including backwardness and inadequate representation.
• The ruling aims to strike a balance between affirmative action for marginalized communities and the principle of administrative efficiency.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for public sector employment policies across various states and central ministries.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court clarified guidelines on reservation in promotions for SC/ST.
Emphasized need for quantifiable data to justify such reservations.
State not bound, but must meet conditions (backwardness, inadequate representation) if provided.
Aims to balance affirmative action and administrative efficiency.
Impacts public sector employment policies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions and judicial pronouncements governing reservation in promotions for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in India.
Discuss the complexities involved in balancing the principles of social justice, administrative efficiency, and meritocracy in public employment.
Examine the socio-economic implications of reservation policies on vulnerable sections and their role in achieving equity.
Critically evaluate the arguments for and against reservation in promotions, considering the need for quantifiable data and periodic review.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial decisions.
GS2.trivia: Article 16(4A) and 16(4B) of the Indian Constitution deal with reservation in promotions for SCs and STs.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Reviews Progress of National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee convened to review the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the integration of vocational education, changes in curriculum design, and the establishment of new regulatory bodies.
• Members expressed concerns regarding funding allocation, teacher training, and equitable access to quality education, especially in remote areas.
• The panel emphasized the need for a phased and inclusive approach to NEP implementation, ensuring no student is left behind.
• Recommendations are expected to be submitted to the government to address bottlenecks and accelerate the policy's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee reviewed NEP 2020 implementation.
Focused on vocational education, curriculum, regulatory bodies.
Concerns raised on funding, teacher training, equitable access.
Emphasized phased and inclusive approach.
Recommendations to address bottlenecks and accelerate NEP objectives.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and their potential impact on India's education system.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education policy like NEP 2020 across diverse states and educational institutions.
Examine the importance of vocational education and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP and enhancing human resource development.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and bridge the learning gap in India, especially in remote and disadvantaged regions.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after policies in 1968 and 1986 (modified in 1992).

GS2.headline: G20 Tourism Ministers Adopt 'Goa Roadmap' for Sustainable Tourism Development
GS2.explainer:
• The G20 Tourism Ministers' Meeting concluded with the adoption of the 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism', focusing on sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
• The roadmap outlines key priorities, including green tourism, digitalization, skill development, and promotion of cultural heritage.
• Ministers emphasized the importance of resilient tourism systems capable of withstanding future shocks and contributing to local economies.
• India, as the host, championed the concept of 'Travel for LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) to promote responsible tourism practices.
• The declaration aims to foster international cooperation to rebuild and reshape the global tourism sector for a more sustainable future.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: G20 Tourism Ministers adopted 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism'.
Focus on sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
Priorities: green tourism, digitalization, skill development, cultural heritage.
India championed 'Travel for LiFE'.
Aims to foster international cooperation for resilient tourism.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the 'Goa Roadmap for Tourism' in promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices globally and for India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging its cultural heritage and natural beauty for sustainable tourism development.
Examine the role of digitalization and skill development in transforming the tourism sector and enhancing its resilience to future crises.
Evaluate the 'Travel for LiFE' initiative and its potential to encourage environmentally conscious tourism behaviors among travelers.
GS2.imageDescription: A vibrant image of a beach in Goa with tourists enjoying, symbolizing tourism and leisure.
GS2.trivia: India hosted the G20 presidency in 2023, during which several key initiatives, including the 'Goa Roadmap', were finalized.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission's Phase II, Targets Energy Security and Decarbonization
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and consumption across key sectors.
• This phase introduces new incentives for green hydrogen production, including viability gap funding for electrolysis and a framework for green hydrogen hubs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy independence and net-zero targets.
• Emphasis is placed on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers and associated supply chain components.
• The policy framework also includes provisions for skill development and research & development in green hydrogen technologies.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched to promote green hydrogen production.
Phase II focuses on scaling production, incentives for electrolysis, and green hydrogen hubs.
Aims to make India a global hub for production and export.
Key components include indigenous manufacturing of electrolysers and R&D.
Union Cabinet approval marks a significant policy milestone.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential of green hydrogen for India, considering job creation, export opportunities, and technological advancements.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the role of government policy and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen refers specifically to hydrogen produced through electrolysis powered by renewable energy.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to inflation management while supporting economic growth, amidst global uncertainties and domestic inflation pressures.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target, while revising GDP growth projections upwards.
• The central bank highlighted risks from volatile global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions, alongside robust domestic demand.
• Experts suggest the hold indicates a period of 'wait and watch' before any potential rate cuts, dependent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Sixth consecutive time the rate has been held.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
