GDP growth projections revised upwards, inflation concerns persist.
Decision influenced by global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic activity.
Examine the impact of global economic headwinds and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving sustained disinflation.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: ISRO Announces Preparations for Chandrayaan-4, Eyes Lunar Sample Return Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) officially announced the commencement of preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis.
• This mission will significantly advance India's capabilities in deep space exploration and planetary science.
• The mission profile is expected to involve a lander, rover, and an ascent module to collect samples, followed by an orbital module for Earth return.
• This follows the successes of Chandrayaan-1, 2, and 3, building on India's growing prowess in lunar exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4 announced by ISRO.
Aims to be a lunar sample return mission.
Will collect lunar soil and rock samples for analysis.
Includes lander, rover, ascent module, and Earth return module.
Follows previous successful Chandrayaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze how Chandrayaan-4 will enhance India's capabilities in deep space exploration and its standing in the global space community.
Examine the technological challenges involved in executing a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is preparing to overcome them.
Evaluate the broader implications of such missions for indigenous technology development and scientific research in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of a lunar lander and rover on the Moon's surface, collecting samples.
GS3.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: the USA, Russia (then Soviet Union), and China.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Expands to Include New Reserve in Eastern Ghats, Boosting Biodiversity Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced the inclusion of a new tiger reserve in the Eastern Ghats region, marking a significant expansion of Project Tiger.
• This new reserve aims to protect a crucial tiger corridor and enhance the conservation of biodiversity in the less-explored Eastern Ghats.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict in existing reserves.
• The new reserve will involve local community participation in conservation efforts and promote eco-tourism sustainably.
• Project Tiger continues to be a flagship conservation program, contributing to a steady increase in India's tiger population.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New tiger reserve established in Eastern Ghats under Project Tiger.
Aims to protect a crucial tiger corridor and biodiversity.
Addresses habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict.
Involves local community participation and sustainable eco-tourism.
Project Tiger is India's flagship tiger conservation program.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of expanding protected areas like tiger reserves for biodiversity conservation in India.
Discuss the ecological significance of the Eastern Ghats as a potential habitat for diverse wildlife, including tigers.
Examine the challenges in managing human-wildlife conflict and ensuring community participation in conservation efforts.
Evaluate the success of Project Tiger in India and suggest future strategies for sustainable wildlife management.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage in a protected reserve.
GS3.trivia: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 and has been instrumental in increasing India's tiger population from a mere 1,800 in the 1970s to over 3,000 currently.

GS3.headline: Indian Army and US Special Forces Conclude Joint Exercise 'Vajra Prahar' in Himachal Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• The 16th edition of the joint military exercise 'Vajra Prahar' between Indian Army Special Forces and US Special Forces concluded in Himachal Pradesh.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability and sharing best practices in counter-terrorism operations, special operations, and airborne assaults.
• It involved complex tactical drills, hostage rescue scenarios, and precision targeting in mountainous terrain.
• 'Vajra Prahar' serves as a crucial platform for strengthening defense cooperation and strategic partnership between India and the United States.
• The exercise underscores the growing convergence of security interests between the two nations in the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: 'Vajra Prahar' is a joint exercise between Indian Army and US Special Forces.
16th edition held in Himachal Pradesh.
Focus on counter-terrorism, special operations, airborne assaults.
Enhances interoperability and defense cooperation.
Strengthens India-US strategic partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of joint military exercises like 'Vajra Prahar' in strengthening India's defense capabilities and fostering international security cooperation.
Discuss how such exercises contribute to enhancing interoperability and sharing best practices in counter-terrorism operations.
Examine the evolving defense partnership between India and the United States and its implications for regional and global security.
Evaluate the role of special forces in modern warfare and their importance in tackling non-traditional security threats.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian and US Special Forces soldiers conducting a tactical drill in mountainous terrain during the 'Vajra Prahar' exercise.
GS3.trivia: The name 'Vajra Prahar' translates to 'Thunderbolt Strike' in Sanskrit.

GS3.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Mega Industrial Corridor Project in Western India, Boosting Manufacturing and Logistics
GS3.explainer:
• A major segment of a new mega industrial corridor project was inaugurated by the Union Minister for Commerce and Industry in Western India.
• The corridor aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, promoting manufacturing, logistics, and job creation in the region.
• It features state-of-the-art infrastructure, including dedicated freight corridors, smart industrial zones, and multimodal logistics hubs.
• The project is part of India's broader strategy to enhance its manufacturing competitiveness and integrate into global supply chains.
• Expected to boost regional economic development and improve ease of doing business for industries.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Mega industrial corridor project inaugurated in Western India.
Aims to attract investment, boost manufacturing and logistics.
Features dedicated freight corridors, smart industrial zones, multimodal hubs.
Part of India's strategy to enhance manufacturing competitiveness.
Expected to boost regional economic development and job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of industrial corridors in driving economic growth, promoting manufacturing, and creating employment opportunities in India.
Analyze the role of infrastructure development in attracting foreign direct investment (FDI) and integrating India into global supply chains.
Examine the challenges and potential benefits of developing mega infrastructure projects, including land acquisition and environmental concerns.
Evaluate the 'Make in India' initiative in the context of industrial corridor development and its impact on manufacturing competitiveness.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a sprawling industrial zone with factories and interconnected roads.
GS3.trivia: India's first industrial corridor was the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC), conceived to create smart industrial cities along the route.

GS3.headline: UN Report Highlights Major Shortfall in Global Climate Finance for Developing Nations
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) warns of a significant shortfall in climate finance for developing countries.
• The report states that developed nations are falling short of their commitment to provide $100 billion annually, hindering climate action in vulnerable regions.
• It emphasizes the urgent need for increased public and private funding to support adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing economies.
• The shortfall disproportionately affects countries most vulnerable to climate impacts, exacerbating existing inequalities.
• Calls for innovative financing mechanisms and greater transparency in climate finance flows ahead of the next COP summit.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNFCCC report highlights shortfall in global climate finance for developing nations.
Developed nations falling short of $100 billion annual commitment.
Urgent need for increased public and private funding.
Disproportionately affects vulnerable countries.
Calls for innovative financing and transparency before next COP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and practical implications of the shortfall in climate finance for developing countries, particularly India.
Analyze the mechanisms and challenges associated with mobilizing and delivering climate finance to vulnerable nations.
Examine India's position and efforts in advocating for greater climate justice and equitable burden-sharing in global climate action.
Suggest innovative financing models and policy reforms to bridge the climate finance gap and accelerate climate action globally.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the gap between committed and delivered climate finance, with scales tipping unevenly.
GS3.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at the 2009 UN Climate Change Conference (COP15) in Copenhagen.

GS3.headline: FAO Food Price Index Sees Modest Rise, Global Food Security Remains Fragile
GS3.explainer:
• The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) released its monthly Food Price Index, indicating a modest rise driven by increases in dairy and sugar prices.
• Despite the slight increase, the overall index remains below its peak levels from previous years, but global food security remains fragile.
• The report highlights ongoing concerns about geopolitical conflicts, climate shocks, and supply chain disruptions affecting food availability and access.
• Developing nations, particularly in Africa and Asia, continue to face acute food insecurity challenges.
• Calls for enhanced international cooperation and resilient agricultural systems to ensure stable food supplies globally.
gsSubTopics: Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: FAO Food Price Index shows modest rise, driven by dairy and sugar.
Global food security remains fragile despite index below peak.
Geopolitical conflicts, climate shocks, supply chain disruptions are concerns.
Developing nations face acute food insecurity.
Calls for international cooperation and resilient agricultural systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the volatility of global food prices and their impact on food security in India and other developing nations.
Discuss the challenges faced by India in ensuring food security for its large population amidst climate change and global supply chain disruptions.
Examine the role of international organizations like FAO in monitoring global food prices and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
Suggest policy interventions at national and international levels to build resilient food systems and address food insecurity effectively.
GS3.imageDescription: Various fresh food items arranged on a table, representing global food supply.
GS3.trivia: The FAO Food Price Index is a measure of the monthly change in international prices of a basket of food commodities.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
